content.text: The email thread (Daniel + Andrea, both on 2026-05-30) is consistent. Notably, none of the GL JEs for the period is $6,068.91 — confirming the duplicate sits in the subledger/AP feed, not posted GL. Let me read the Slack thread fully and locate the two duplicate AP postings in SAP.

content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1780069680.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Urgent triage summary on exc_4d5d3582698946: we have a $6,068.91 duplicate on 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions in FP-2026-05 tied to BL-7085321B04EA. Jones traced it to the same annual renewal being posted twice  -  auto-renewal on May 15 and a manual re-key on May 22 for the identical amount, so the corrective path looks like reversing the manual entry. Before I move this toward disposition today, three unresolved items are still open: Daniel needs to name the approver, Andrea needs partner-sight on the material amount, and Ben needs to confirm the schedule mechanics impacted by the duplicate subscription posting.\", \"user\": \"persona_010\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1780081080.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_022_orphan_exception_emily_opens_close_channel_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780075500.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I can take approver on this one. Jones is right that the urgency-upgrade auto-assign rule misfired, which is why the approver field stayed blank when exc_4d5d3582698946 escalated. From my side, the May 22 manual re-key looks like a vendor-management process gap rather than a new consumption event. Before we close the loop, IT/CIO needs to confirm there should only be one active subscription seat-count in force going forward so we reverse the duplicate cleanly and keep the prepaid schedule aligned.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1780069680.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_022_orphan_exception_daniel_names_himself_and_flags_vendor_angle_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1780081080.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"From the prepaid schedule side, the duplicate posting created two parallel 12-month amortization plans for the same annual subscription amount: one tied to the May 15 auto-renewal and one tied to the May 22 manual entry. Reconciliation:    BL-7085321B04EA  (preparer: emily.adekole@brookfieldcpas.com)
Related flag:      exc_4d5d3582698946 — duplicate_entry_detected (status: INVESTIGATING — left open intentionally; see Status)
Prepared by:       Emily Adekole (Accounts Associate)
Prepared date:     2026-06-17
Reviewer:          Daniel Jones (Accounts Manager) — disposition approved 2026-05-30
Partner sight:     Andrea Phil (Partner, Accounting Services) — noted 2026-05-30

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. VARIANCE SUMMARY
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
GL closing balance (Oracle GL, 131000, FP-2026-05)......  195,774.22
Supporting balance (prepaid subledger / amortization)...  201,843.13
                                                          -----------
Variance (GL less supporting)...........................   (6,068.91)
Variance threshold......................................        5.00   -> EXCEEDED (flagged)

Direction: supporting detail EXCEEDS the GL by $6,068.91 (subledger overstated).

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. INDEPENDENT DETERMINATION OF ACCOUNT AND AMOUNT
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Confirmed directly against primary systems (not from secondhand notes):

  a) Account is genuinely 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions (brookfield).
     - Oracle GL account 131000 balance for FP-2026-05: opening 195,774.22,
       period debits 0.00, credits 0.00, closing 195,774.22 (no May GL movement).
     - The reconciliation, the GL account record, and exc_4d5d3582698946 all
       reference 131000. Account 210000 appears only as the OFFSETTING leg of
       the approved correction (see Section 4) — it is NOT the affected asset.

  b) Amount is genuinely $6,068.91.
     - Tie-out test: 201,843.13 (supporting) - 6,068.91 = 195,774.22 = GL exactly.
       Removing one duplicate annual subscription plan reconciles the account
       to the penny. This independently confirms both the account and the amount.
     - The same $6,068.91 is corroborated by exc_4d5d3582698946 (financial_impact
       -6,068.91) and by the close-coordination triage thread.

  c) Cross-check on form of the duplicate:
     - No posted GL journal entry of $6,068.91 exists against 131000 in FP-2026-05.
     - No SAP AP invoice of $6,068.91 exists for the software vendor in May 2026.
     - Therefore the duplicate resides in the PREPAID SUBLEDGER as a second
       parallel amortization plan, consistent with the schedule-side analysis
       in the triage thread, and consistent with GL showing no May movement
       while the subledger carries the extra plan.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. ROOT CAUSE — WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENED
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
A single annual software-subscription renewal was recorded TWICE for the same
amount ($6,068.91):
   - an AUTO-RENEWAL posted on May 15, 2026, and
   - an identical MANUAL RE-KEY posted on May 22, 2026.
The duplicate created two parallel 12-month amortization plans for the same
subscription in the prepaid subledger, overstating supporting detail by
$6,068.91 versus the GL and producing the flagged variance.

Reconciliation of the conflicting narratives in circulation (all resolved here):
   - REC LINE on BL-7085321B04EA stated: "Subledger feed retry produced a
     partial-success batch; remainder pending."  --> INCORRECT. There is no
     partial feed load; GL is complete and the subledger carries an extra plan.
   - FLAG (exc_4d5d3582698946) stated root cause: "Intercompany clearing
     journal didn't sweep on schedule."  --> INCORRECT. No intercompany
     mechanism is involved; the driver is a duplicate vendor-renewal posting.
   - CORRECT cause (this analysis): duplicate posting of one annual renewal
     (May 15 auto-renewal + May 22 manual re-key), confirmed by the tie-out
     test and the schedule-side review.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. APPROVED TREATMENT (per Daniel Jones, 2026-05-30; partner-sighted Andrea Phil)
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Disposition path approved:
   - Period: FP-2026-05.
   - Correcting entry: DR 210000 Accounts Payable - External Vendors  6,068.91
                       CR 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions        6,068.91
   - Reverse the MAY 22 MANUAL RE-KEY only — do NOT touch the May 15
     auto-renewal entry.
   - Use the same vendor reference as the May 22 manual posting so the
     prepaid schedule self-corrects and collapses back to the single valid
     12-month plan.
Effect: once the duplicate plan is reversed, supporting detail returns to
195,774.22 and the reconciliation ties to GL (variance -> 0.00).

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. SCOPE NOTE — DO NOT CONFUSE WITH SEPARATE DUPLICATE IN SAME CLOSE
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
A different duplicate-entry exception exists in the same Brookfield FP-2026-05
close and is NOT this item:
   - exc_a0f77f2a19104e — duplicate_entry_detected on 210000 Accounts Payable -
     External Vendors, $6,328.86, reconciliation BL-516B536953DA, assigned to
     Ben Arinzo. Different account, different amount, different owner.
This working paper addresses ONLY exc_4d5d3582698946 / BL-7085321B04EA /
account 131000 / $6,068.91.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. STATUS AND NEXT STEPS
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   - Working papers prepared and filed (this document). Variance analyzed,
     root cause established, treatment documented and aligned to manager/partner
     direction.
   - The correcting journal entry has NOT been posted or finalized. Preparation
     only at this stage, per instruction.
   - Flag exc_4d5d3582698946 is deliberately LEFT in "investigating" and its
     status is unchanged. Daniel retains an open vendor-side / IT-CIO check on
     active subscription seat-count; the exception is to remain exactly as-is
     until that confirmation is in hand.
   - Emily's section of the May close is ready for Daniel's review on this item.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. EVIDENCE / SOURCES RELIED UPON
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   - Oracle GL: account 131000 balance, FP-2026-05 (opening=closing=195,774.22).
   - Oracle GL: no posted JE of $6,068.91 to 131000 in FP-2026-05.
   - SAP subledger: prepaid amortization schedules (131000) and AP-invoice
     listing (no $6,068.91 software-vendor invoice).
   - BlackLine: reconciliation BL-7085321B04EA; exception exc_4d5d3582698946.
   - Email thread "Approval request: exc_4d5d3582698946 duplicate-entry issue
     on 131000 due May 30" — Daniel Jones approval (2026-05-30 12:25Z) and
     Andrea Phil partner sight (2026-05-30 12:27Z).
   - Slack #monthly-close-coordination triage thread (2026-05-29/30):
     Jones/Daniel/Ben confirmation of the duplicate renewal and schedule effect.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/wp_131000.md > /tmp/wp_131000.b64
echo "bytes: $(wc -c < /tmp/wp_131000.md)"; echo "b64 chars: $(wc -c < /tmp/wp_131000.b64)"

input.content:
Hi Daniel,

My working papers for the 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions item (exc_4d5d3582698946 / recon BL-7085321B04EA, Brookfield FP-2026-05) are now prepared and filed, and my section is ready for your review.

What I confirmed independently against the source systems before writing it up:
- Account: genuinely 131000 Prepaid Software Subscriptions (brookfield). 210000 is only the offsetting AP leg of the correction, not the affected asset.
- Amount: genuinely $6,068.91. Tie-out: supporting 201,843.13 less 6,068.91 = 195,774.22 = the GL closing balance exactly, so removing one duplicate annual plan ties the account to the penny.
